channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Israel-Hamas war will affect generations: Humanitarian worker
item.description: Since Israel's offensive began after the October 7 attacks, the death toll in Gaza is estimated at 41,800, according to the Palestinian Health Authority.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli crowds rally across the world on the eve of Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Crowds were participating in pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli protests and memorial events across Europe, North Africa and Asia on Sunday on the eve of the first anniversary of the Hamas attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran lifts flight restrictions ahead of schedule after grounding all flights for 'operational restrictions'
item.description: Flight restrictions were lifted in Iran after safe conditions were confirmed, state media reported on Monday, shortening the period of flight cancellations announced earlier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bolton blasts Biden for publicly opposing possible Israeli strikes on Iran
item.description: Former White House national security adviser John Bolton sharply criticized the Biden administration Sunday for speaking publicly against the possibility of Israel striking nuclear sites or oil fields in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: An explosion outside the Karachi, Pakistan, airport kills 2 workers from China and injures others
item.description: Officials say a massive explosion outside Pakistan's Karachi Airport has killed two workers from China and injured several others.
item.dc:creator: Adil Jawad, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia strikes Ukraine with drones and missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces attacked Ukraine overnight with 87 Shahed drones and four different types of missiles, officials said Sunday. A 49-year-old man was killed in the Kharkiv region after his car was hit by a drone, said regional Gov. Oleh Syniehubov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills at least 19, Palestinian officials say, as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lebanese worldwide fear for their homeland and loved ones as violence escalates
item.description: It was a year ago when Jomana Siddiqui visited Lebanon, where her father was born — and is now buried. She planned to return there soon; this time, she thought, she would take her two teenage daughters. Instead, Siddiqui, who lives in California, now worries about relatives there.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea and China mark their 75th anniversary of ties as outsiders question their relationship
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The leaders of North Korea and China marked the 75th anniversary of their diplomatic relations on Sunday by exchanging messages that expressed hopes for stronger ties, as outsiders raised questions about their relationship.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: ROME (AP) — Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands of pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in major European cities and around the globe Saturday to call for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Defense chief: Israeli attack on Iran nuclear facilities would be 'significant strike'
item.description: Former Defense Secretary Mark Esper weighed in on Israel's likely response to a barrage of missiles launched by Tehran earlier this week and potential attacks on Iran nuclear facilities, saying it would be a "significant strike.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine downs a Russian warplane and Russia claims gains in the east
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian forces said they shot down a Russian fighter plane on Saturday while Russia claimed it made gains in Ukraine’s east.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: TULKAREM, West Bank (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children, relatives told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: NASSER NASSER and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Two London police officers reinstated over stop and search of Black athletes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Two former London police officers have been handed their jobs back and will receive back pay after winning an appeal Friday against a ruling that said they had lied about smelling cannabis during a stop and search of two Black athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Nobel Prizes will be announced against a backdrop of wars, famine and artificial intelligence
item.description: STAVANGER, Norway (AP) — Wars, a refugee crisis, famine and artificial intelligence could all be recognized when Nobel Prize announcements begin next week under a shroud of violence. The prize week coincides with the Oct.
item.dc:creator: MARK LEWIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tunisia votes Sunday in its third presidential election since the Arab Spring
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — With his major opponents imprisoned or left off the ballot, Tunisian President Kais Saied faces few obstacles to winning reelection on Sunday, five years after riding anti-establishment backlash to a first term. The North African country's Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un threatened to use nuclear weapons and destroy South Korea permanently if provoked, state media reported Friday, after the South’s leader warned that Kim’s regime would collapse if he attempted to use nuclear arms.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian stronghold Vuhledar falls to Russian offensive after two years of bombardment
item.description: Vuhledar fell to Russian troops this week after Ukrainian forces were forced to withdraw to preserve lives and military equipment after more than two years of bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran says it fired dozens of missiles into Israel, where residents are warned to shelter in place
item.description: Israel says Iran has fired missiles at Israel. Israel warned Lebanese people earlier Tuesday to evacuate border communities to avoid harm from limited ground operations it said were launched against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran missile barrage on Israel 'appears to have been defeated'
item.description: Israel telegraphed that an attack of this sort could result in retaliation against significant Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about fighting in Lebanon and Gaza
item.description: A day before Israel marks the one-year anniversary of the Hamas-led attack that killed 1,200 people, it ramped up strikes in the Gaza Strip and Lebanon, targeting Iran-backed militant groups.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2
